meta_title: For Freelancers: API integration guide to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelance API Integration Guide: Overcome No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Master API integration to bypass design hurdles. A professional, intermediate-level checklist for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship fast.
content.answer_block: Stop fighting CSS frameworks. Use API-first development to build functional, high-value SaaS products. By leveraging headless UI libraries and automated code generation via Antigravity and Claude Code, you can deliver professional-grade client applications that focus on logic and data flow, completely bypassing the need for custom design work.
content.intro:
The client meeting went well, but the reality of the build is staring you down. You are a developer, not a designer, and the thought of pixel-perfecting a dashboard is stalling your velocity. Every hour spent wrestling with margins is an hour lost on high-value logic and client deliverables.

But here is the secret: sophisticated clients don't pay for CSS; they pay for functionality that solves their business problems. By adopting an API-first integration strategy, you can leverage robust design systems and automated scaffolding to ship production-ready apps that look professional by default. It is time to stop building from scratch and start orchestrating.

steps.title: Define the Data Schema
steps.description: Before touching code, map out your API endpoints. Focus on the data lifecycle—how information moves from the user to the database and back.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust JSON schema for a SaaS dashboard handling user profiles and subscription metrics. Ensure all fields are typed for TypeScript integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a schema.ts file defining the core data structures based on the provided API documentation.'

steps.title: Bootstrap with Headless UI
steps.description: Avoid custom styling by using a component library that handles accessibility and responsiveness out of the box.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a React project using Tailwind CSS and Shadcn/UI for a clean, professional enterprise dashboard aesthetic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Initialize project with Shadcn/UI components for table, card, and navigation layouts.'

steps.title: Implement API Layer
steps.description: Create a centralized service file to manage API calls, ensuring clean error handling and type-safe responses.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a reusable API service class using Axios with interceptors for auth-token injection and centralized error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create src/services/api.ts and implement a generic fetch wrapper with error logging.'

steps.title: Automate View Binding
steps.description: Connect your UI components to the API layer, transforming data into meaningful visuals without manual layout work.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a data-grid component that maps API response objects to a responsive table layout with loading states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Map the API response to the Shadcn table component and implement a loading skeleton state.'

steps.title: Rigorous Integration Testing
steps.description: Validate that the API data flows correctly into the UI under edge cases like empty states or network latency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of integration tests for the dashboard components using Vitest, mocking API responses to simulate network errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create integration tests in src/tests/dashboard.spec.ts to verify component data rendering.'

pro_tips: Always prioritize 'API-first' architecture; if the data is structured correctly, the UI is just a thin, interchangeable layer.
Use 'Component Libraries' like Shadcn/UI to avoid writing custom CSS; they are the industry standard for professional, low-effort design.
Implement 'Optimistic UI' updates to make your app feel faster than it is—crucial for impressing clients during demos.
Leverage 'Claude Code' to write your unit tests; it reduces the tedious burden of manual test maintenance by 80%.
Keep your API service layer decoupled from your UI; this allows you to swap out design systems in the future without breaking logic.
resources: TanStack Query for API State: https://tanstack.com/query/latest
content.next_steps: Once the integration is functional, focus on deploying to a platform like Vercel and setting up a CI/CD pipeline to automate your production releases.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting design friction slow down your freelance business. Use these tools to ship faster, earn more, and build with the confidence of a lead engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
